name: redis-rate-limit
description: v0.5.2 middleware — fixed-window rate limiter backed by Redis. Mirrors `rate-limit.json` step-for-step but swaps `@blokjs/in-memory-kv` for `@blokjs/redis-kv`, so counters survive process restarts and synchronize across replicas (the in-memory variant is process-local — fine for single-process dev, wrong for production behind a load balancer). The bucket key is `ctx.state.identity.token` if an auth middleware ran first, falling back to the X-Forwarded-For header. Demo defaults: 5 requests per 60s window. Connects via REDIS_URL env (default redis://127.0.0.1:6379). Per-key TTL is set via Redis SET ... PX <windowMs> so expired buckets evict automatically — no background reaper needed.

inputs.expression: ((b) => { const now = Date.now(); const limit = 5; const windowMs = 60000; if (!b || typeof b.expiresAt !== 'number' || b.expiresAt <= now) { return { count: 1, expiresAt: now + windowMs, exceeded: false, retryAfterSec: 0, ttlMs: windowMs }; } if (b.count + 1 > limit) { return { count: b.count, expiresAt: b.expiresAt, exceeded: true, retryAfterSec: Math.max(1, Math.ceil((b.expiresAt - now) / 1000)), ttlMs: Math.max(1, b.expiresAt - now) }; } return { count: b.count + 1, expiresAt: b.expiresAt, exceeded: false, retryAfterSec: 0, ttlMs: Math.max(1, b.expiresAt - now) }; })((ctx.state['load-bucket'] && ctx.state['load-bucket'].value) || null)
